data.title: Uber Leverages Amazon's AI Technology for Enhanced Services
data.description: Uber is integrating Amazon's custom AI chips and AWS infrastructure to enhance its AI models and support real-time services for millions of daily users. According to NS3.AI, Uber is increasingly utilizing AWS Graviton4 for more Trip Serving Zone workloads. Additionally, the company has initiated pilot training on Trainium to optimize rider and delivery matching.

data.title: Dormant Miner Sells 265.19 BTC After Two-Year Hiatus
data.description: A dormant Bitcoin miner has recently sold 265.19 BTC, valued at $18.06 million. According to NS3.AI, this transaction marks the first sale from the wallet in two years, indicating a significant move in the cryptocurrency market.

data.title: US-Iran Negotiations Show Progress Amid Ceasefire Deadline
data.description: US-Iran talks have made significant progress over the past 24 hours. According to NS3.AI, despite the advancements, a ceasefire before U.S. President Donald Trump's 8 PM ET deadline remains improbable.

data.title: Federal Reserve Official Comments on Economic Conditions
data.description: Federal Reserve official Goolsbee has stated that while the current job market remains stable, the overall economic situation is not favorable. According to ChainCatcher, Goolsbee's remarks highlight concerns about the broader economic landscape despite steady employment figures.

data.title: Federal Reserve's Goolsbee Warns of Inflation Resurgence
data.description: Federal Reserve official Austan Goolsbee has issued a warning about the potential resurgence of inflation. According to Jin10, Goolsbee expressed concerns that inflation could make a comeback, impacting economic stability. He emphasized the importance of monitoring inflationary trends closely to ensure appropriate policy responses. Goolsbee's remarks come amid ongoing discussions about the Federal Reserve's monetary policy and its efforts to balance economic growth with inflation control. The statement highlights the challenges faced by policymakers in navigating the complex economic landscape.

data.title: Sources say that negotiations between the United States and Iran have made progress in the past 24 hours.
data.description: US and Israeli officials and sources say that negotiations between the US and Iran have made progress in the past 24 hours, but the likelihood of reaching a ceasefire agreement before Trump's 8 p.m. Eastern Time deadline still seems slim. (Jinshi)

data.title: A miner sold 265.19 BTC after two years of inactivity, worth $18.06 million.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, miner “3PFNdg” sold 265.19 BTC worth $18.06 million one hour ago. It is reported that the last time this miner’s wallet sold Bitcoin was two years ago.

data.title: EIA Forecasts Prolonged Middle East Oil Supply Disruptions Until 2026
data.description:
The Energy Information Administration (EIA) has projected that disruptions in oil supply from the Middle East will continue until the end of 2026. According to NS3.AI, the closure of the Strait of Hormuz is expected to result in a loss of 9.1 million barrels per day by April. The EIA report also indicates that the Brent-WTI crude spread could reach a peak of $15 per barrel in April as these disruptions intensify.

Additionally, the EIA forecasts that U.S. retail gasoline prices will average their highest levels since 2022 by 2026. The report also includes a downward revision of global oil demand forecasts for 2026 and 2027.

data.title: BlackRock Withdraws Significant Cryptocurrency Holdings from CEX
data.description: On April 8, BlackRock made a substantial withdrawal of cryptocurrency assets from a centralized exchange (CEX). According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the financial giant extracted 2,607 BTC and 28,391 ETH from the platform within a 30-minute timeframe. This move highlights BlackRock's active engagement in the cryptocurrency market and its strategic asset management decisions.

data.title: The Pump.fun team/investor-related address deposited 2.34 billion PUMPs into CEX.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens, the Pump.fun team/investor-related address deposited 2.34 billion PUMPs into the cryptocurrency exchange Bitget, worth approximately $4 million.

data.title: EIA Report: Iranian Situation Shifts Market Dynamics to Short-Term Supply Tightness
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) has released its Short-Term Energy Outlook report, highlighting a shift in market dynamics due to the situation in Iran. According to Jin10, the report indicates that the market has moved from an expectation of oversupply to concerns over short-term supply tightness. This change is attributed to geopolitical tensions in the region, which have impacted oil supply forecasts. The EIA's analysis suggests that these developments could influence global energy markets in the near term.

data.title: FOMC Member and Chicago Fed President Goolsbee to Speak on Monetary Policy
data.description: FOMC member and Chicago Federal Reserve President Austan Goolsbee is scheduled to deliver a speech on monetary policy in ten minutes. According to Jin10, this address is anticipated to provide insights into the Federal Reserve's current stance and future direction regarding interest rates and economic strategy. Market participants are closely watching for any indications of potential shifts in policy that could impact financial markets.

data.title: Bloomberg analyst: Musk may use SPCX as SpaceX's IPO ticker symbol.
data.description: Eric Balchunas, a senior ETF analyst at Bloomberg, stated on the X platform that Musk is likely to use SPCX as the ticker symbol for SpaceX's IPO. This symbol was previously held by Matt Tuttle, but the relevant ETFs have already changed their ticker symbols. Balchunas pointed out that the Bloomberg ETF team predicted this as early as last December, and the discovery of the SpaceX IPO ticker symbol "SPCX" was made by Will Hershey, co-founder of Roundhill Investments, who had previously "given" his META ticker to Mark Zuckerberg, calling it "a tacit understanding among peers."

data.title: On April 7th, US Bitcoin ETFs saw a net inflow of 6,665 BTC, and Ethereum ETFs saw a net inflow of 39,768 ETH.
data.description: According to Lookonchain monitoring, on April 7, the US Bitcoin ETF saw a net inflow of 6,665 BTC, the Ethereum ETF saw a net inflow of 39,768 ETH, and the Solana ETF saw a net inflow of 2,919 SOL.

data.title: BlackRock withdrew 2,607 BTC and 28,391 ETH from Coinbase Prime
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock just withdrew 2,607 BTC (worth $177.56 million) and 28,391 ETH (worth $59 million) from a Coinbase Prime address.

data.title: EIA Short-Term Energy Outlook Report: Middle East supply disruptions are expected to continue until the end of 2026.
data.description: The EIA's Short-Term Energy Outlook report indicates that Middle East supply disruptions are expected to continue until the end of 2026. Oil production cuts in the Middle East due to the closure of the Strait of Hormuz are projected to increase to 9.1 million barrels per day in April. The Brent-WTI crude oil price spread is expected to peak at $15 per barrel in April, at which point Middle East oil supply disruptions will reach their maximum. US retail gasoline prices are projected to reach their highest average level since 2022 in 2026. Global oil demand is projected at 104.6 million barrels per day in 2026, lower than the previous forecast of 105.2 million barrels per day; demand is projected at 106.2 million barrels per day in 2027, also lower than the previous forecast of 106.6 million barrels per day. (Jinshi)

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs Experience Slowdown Amid Regulatory Uncertainty
data.description: XRP spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) currently manage approximately $1 billion in assets. According to NS3.AI, inflows for 2026 have significantly decelerated, with March witnessing net outflows of $28 million. On Monday, XRP was trading at around $1.30, marking a decline of about 40% from its peak in January. A key factor to monitor is the upcoming CLARITY Act, which aims to establish a U.S. digital asset framework and could influence the resurgence of institutional demand.

data.title: EIA Revises U.S. Natural Gas Production Forecasts for 2026 and 2027
data.description: The U.S. Energy Information Administration (EIA) has updated its short-term energy outlook, adjusting its forecasts for U.S. natural gas production. According to Jin10, the EIA now projects that U.S. dry natural gas production will reach 1096.0 billion cubic feet per day in 2026, slightly up from the previous forecast of 1095.0 billion cubic feet per day. For 2027, the EIA anticipates production will increase to 1126.0 billion cubic feet per day, compared to the earlier estimate of 1123.0 billion cubic feet per day. These revisions reflect the EIA's ongoing assessment of market conditions and production capabilities.

data.title: A newly created wallet withdrew 300 BTC from Binance, worth $20.43 million.
data.description: According to Onchain Lens monitoring, a newly created wallet just withdrew 300 BTC from Binance, worth $20.43 million.

data.title: Bitcoin Active Addresses Reach Lowest Level Since 2026
data.description:
Bitcoin's active addresses have dropped to their lowest point since 2026, marking an eight-year low, according to BlockBeats. Analyst Gaah suggests that the current market environment, characterized by low volatility and speculative activity, is ideal for 'smart money' and institutional investors to accumulate large positions without causing significant price fluctuations.

The decline in activity indicates that short-term investors, who typically buy on hype and sell in panic, have largely exited the market. The remaining participants are primarily long-term holders who are in the process of accumulating.

Historically, low points in active address metrics often align with the most profitable long-term bottoming phases. The current 'silence' in the network suggests that the available supply is being absorbed by the market.
